mode_selection_reason: Pre-flight Operator Directive Discovery found 1 pending directive (priority=medium). Directives override Mode 0 and Mode 1-4 selection per dispatcher rules. Mode 0 assigned-to-tinker scan returned 0 items; mode quick-checks not run.
previous_followup: Not evaluated this run — directive-driven runs skip the standard PROP-lifecycle walk. Will be picked up by the next non-directive Mode 1/3 run.

backlog_trend.note: Backlog healthy. The 2026-05-31T13:00Z operator-burndown of 18 stuck ISSs (commit 7ec29efbbd) is reflected in closed_issues_velocity_7d. assigned_analyst dropped from 35 (prior run) to 22 (this run) for the same reason. Net velocity +111 is the strongest reading in recent history.

findings.title: PROP-070 authored per DIRECTIVE-20260531-009
findings.summary: Authored monitor/tinker/proposals/PROP-070-burndown-consolidation-chain-fix.json with status='design-pending-operator-review'. Directive flipped to status='completed' with prop_id_authored='PROP-070'.
findings.directive_framing_correction: The directive hypothesized 'PROP-056 burndown does not traverse consolidation chains.' Investigation showed: (a) PROP-056 is NOT a burndown — it is the status='closed' residue migration sweep; (b) PROP-058 Step A0b IS a recurring burndown but only targets status='blocked-on-curmudgeon', not status='assigned-analyst' (which all 18 stuck ISSs were); (c) the canonical close-on-EXP-integration is Step 8 in decider-curmudgeon-pq-mechanics.md, which DOES carry consolidation chains correctly via the issue_ids aggregation that analyst-baby builds at consolidation time; (d) the DIRECT case (6 of 18) splits into 4 cases where the EXP was not yet integrated (operator burndown was early but defensible) and 2 cases (ISS-2326/2327→EXP-464/465) where commit 1727ee3 integrated the EXPs but Step 8 was bypassed (canonical-path-failure). The CONSOLIDATED case (12 of 18) is correctly architected: chain endpoints EXP-517/518/519/520 carry the aggregated issue_ids and would close all 12 upstream ISSs when Step 8 fires at their integration time — they just haven't been integrated yet.
findings.proposed_fix_summary: Step A0c sweep (sibling to PROP-056 A0 and PROP-058 A0b) targeting status='assigned-analyst' with consolidation-chain walk + amendment-noted hold-back; Step 8 self-test (fail-loud-abort if integrated=true with leaked issue_ids); tinker Mode 2 observability counter.

findings.title: Backlog healthy, no thresholds fired
findings.summary: Net velocity +111 (closed 163 vs new 52 over last 7d). No age_ge_14d entries. open_issues_total at 52 — well below moderate (200) and major (300) thresholds. Operator-burndown 2026-05-31T13:00Z accounts for the drop from 62 (pre-burndown) to 44 (post-burndown); this run's read of 52 is post 6 additional new ISSs created by decider runs at 12-28 and 14-37.

claude_md_audit.reason: Directive-driven run; CLAUDE.md audit deferred to next Mode 3 invocation.

context_load.reason: Directive-driven run; per-agent context load survey deferred to next Mode 3 invocation.

next_run_recommendations: If operator approves PROP-070, follow-up tinker Mode 4 (with self-apply empowerment) can deploy the three files_to_modify items. Otherwise operator applies from a clean clone.
Mode 3 audit (CLAUDE.md accuracy + per-agent context load + no-op rates) is overdue; next non-directive run should default to Mode 3.

Open issue follow-up: ISS-2326 and ISS-2327 (the Step 8 bypass cases on EXP-464/465) were closed by the operator-burndown today, but the underlying Step 8 reliability issue persists until PROP-070 lands. If another decider integration happens before PROP-070 deploys, watch for the same pattern in the next-day audit.
